task.headline: Same enterprise escalation. Same Codex. Same acceptance test. Different schema tax.
task.request: Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow. Detect a delayed high-value order for a strategic account, combine CRM context, open support cases, invoice/payment risk, contract tier, recent product usage, shipment status, regulatory flags, and audit history. Route the account to Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support, show the customer-safe status in the portal, create internal owner tasks, and preserve a full audit timeline.

designReview.reviewer: Rubric-based Independent Design Review for Seller V1
designReview.mongoRationale: The MongoDB target models the account as the Customer 360 aggregate for the hot escalation path: contacts, contract, health, account team, billing summary, usage summary, support summary, compliance summary, current escalation, and task summary are co-located because the workflow reads them together. The order remains an aggregate for line items, fulfillment, payment, and bounded status history. Unbounded streams such as support cases, invoices, usage snapshots, work items, customer escalations, and audit events remain separate collections. This follows MongoDB access-pattern modeling without recreating relational table boundaries.
designReview.postgresRationale: The Postgres target uses a credible normalized relational baseline with explicit tables and foreign keys for account tiers, accounts, contacts, contracts, team members, risk flags, health factors, products, orders, shipments, support cases, invoices, payment risk events, usage snapshots, compliance reviews, escalation policies, escalation cases, risk factors, owner tasks, task assignments, portal messages, legal and finance requests, status history, audit events, SLA, inventory, renewals, and notification outbox. This is a normal relational customer-360 design, not an intentionally weakened schema.
designReview.tradeoff: The comparison is database-native rather than schema-identical. Identical schemas would be less fair because they would punish one database for the other's natural shape. The proof therefore compares the same customer request, same fixture, same acceptance contract, and same outcome while allowing each target to use its idiomatic data model.

evidenceLedger.claim: Captured Codex trace, inspected files, retries, token estimates, and code diffs
Independent design review by MongoDB and Postgres rubric
MongoDB local database seed and workflow replay
Postgres local database seed and workflow replay
Evidence manifest locks verified source files with SHA-256

caveat: Verified for one Codex run pair using generated Customer 360 Escalation records seeded into real local MongoDB and Postgres services. Claude Code, Cursor, and Enterprise SQL Sprawl are future validation lanes and are not claimed by this replay.
